ocr.text: The data presented in Table 1 provides a comprehensive overview of the statistical parameters for Group I, highlighting key
metrics that are essential for understanding the distribution and central tendency of the observed variables. The table includes
critical values such as the Minimum and Maximum, which delineate the range of the data set, thereby offering insights into the
variability within Group I. Notably, the Mean ± SD (Standard Deviation) further illustrates the average performance of the

group while accounting for dispersion, allowing for a deeper analysis of the data's reliability and consistency. 

This information
is particularly relevant for researchers and practitioners seeking to benchmark the performance of individuals within this group
against established norms or to identify potential areas for intervention. By examining these metrics, stakeholders can better
appreciate the underlying trends and patterns that emerge from the data, ultimately informing strategic planning and decision-
making processes. Furthermore, the clarity and precision of these statistics serve to reinforce the validity of the findings,
ensuring that they are grounded in a robust analytical framework. As such, Table 1 is an indispensable resource for
understanding the characteristics of Group I and its implications in the broader context of the study.
